rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE MATTER OF THE MARCUS DE SIQUEIRA NETO, A MINOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #9, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/08/2020. A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: 1) The order approving the prior account required a subsequent account covering a period through 11/30/2019, but the ending date of the final account is 10/09/2019. 2) Paragraph 9 of the final account alleges that the trust estate is set to receive annuity payments, but the annuity is not listed as an asset of the trust, nor are the annuity payments which started in August 2019 reported as receipts. 3) Did the petitioner consult with the Trust Advisory Committee before making the disbursements reported in the final account in accordance with Article 6, Section 7, of the governing trust instrument? 4) Paragraph 20 of the final account and paragraph 6 of the prayer for relief must provide a specific description of property to be distributed to the beneficiary (if cash, a fixed amount as of a date certain). 5) While the final account requests a reserve of $10,000, paragraph 7 of the proposed order provides for a reserve of $6,000. It is also unclear if the petitioner is requesting authority to pay final trustee fees to herself from the reserve without prior court approval. 6) Exhibit C indicates that the beneficiary signed the election to revoke the trust one day after he received a copy of the trust agreement. Was the beneficiary provided with a Portuguese translation of those documents? Note: The petitioner and her attorney need to comply with CRC 1.201 to protect personal privacy. Only the last 4 digits of financial account numbers may be disclosed in pleadings including e-filed account statements (viewable to the public in PES & PTR cases unless sealed).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTY RAMOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/08/2020, as a supplement, signed and verified by the petitioner, addressing the following issues, was not filed by noon on 03/23/2020 in response to the preliminary tentative ruling: 1) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA action reportable under CRC 7.250? 2) There is no allegation as to whether the estate is solvent as required by CRC 7.550(b)(5), even when an account has been waived. 3) There is no allegation as to whether Probate Code section 1064(a)(1)-(3) are applicable to this estate. 4) Has the petitioner given notice to any pubic entity listed in Probate Code section 9201? 5) Is the petitioner the only beneficiary of the devisee trust entitled to notice within the meaning of Probate Code section 1208(b)? 6) The petition only alleges the amount of the assets on hand. A specific description of the assets on hand is required. CRC 7.651. If cash, "as of a date certain" must be alleged. LRSF 14.67(L)(7)(a). 8) No proposed distribution schedule was attached, even though paragraph 1 of the prayer for relief refers to the "attached schedule of distribution".

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLES L. JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/22/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No Declaration of Real Property (local court form) has been filed. 2) The date of mailing in the proof of service of notice of the hearing (filed 02/05/2020) is incomplete. 3) The proof of service was signed before the petition was filed. Notice must be mailed after the filing. LRSF 14.21A. An amended proof of service must be filed. 4) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The box "Authorization to Administer Under the Independent Administration of Estates Act" in the caption was not checked, while item 2c of the petition requests such authority. B) Item 1 of the petition must specify the newspaper name. C) According to item 3d of the petition, the estimated value of the estate property is $3,500 without any real property. What is the purpose of opening this probate in lieu of using the affidavit procedure for collection or transfer of personal property provided by Probate Code sections 13100 et seq.? D) Item 3f(2) alleges the date of execution of the will. The will appears to be dated 07/28/2015. E) The box 3g(1)(a) was not checked. F) The box 6b was not checked, even though item 8 lists the decedent's sisters. G) Item 7 must be completed. H) Item 8 refers to Lolita "Watkins", while the will and her resignation refer to Lolita "Wilkins". I) Is either the petitioner or Sonja Jones a parent of Lolita Wilkins? J) Item 8 of the petition must list the name, relationship, age, and address of the trustee of the Charles L. Jones Trust dated 02/13/2012. If the petitioner is the sole trustee, all beneficiaries of the trust entitled to notice under Probate Code section 1208(b) must be listed in addition and served with notice of the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEMMA RUTH PAYBARAH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/22/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No Declaration of Real Property (local court form) has been filed. LRSF 14.27(C). 2) Notice to each minor beneficiary of the devisee trust must be sent directly to the minor. CRC 7.51(d). Notice to c/o is insufficient. CRC 7.51(a)(2). A separate copy of the notice must be sent to the person(s) having custody of and residing with the minor pursuant to CRC 7.51(d). A supplemental proof of service must be filed. 3) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Item 8 of the petition describes some individuals as "remote" beneficiaries of the devisee trust. An additional explanation as to their "remote" interests is required. Are they remainder beneficiaries unconditionally entitled to distributions after the death of the petitioner as the current sole beneficiary? B) The person or persons having legal custody of and residing with the minor trust beneficiaries within the meaning of CRC 7.51(d) must be identified. C) Item 8 of the petition describes some individuals as "temporary guardians for minors". Have they been appointed as such by any court? D) Should the Court appoint guardians ad litem for the minor trust beneficiaries? E) The address of Anjelica Leila Sarvghadi listed in item 8 of the petition is different from the address listed in the will and proof of service. Was she properly served with notice? F) The addresses of Alfred L. DeLeon and Katherine C. DeLeon listed in item 8 of the petition are different from the addresses listed in the will. Were they properly served with notice? G) According to item 3d of the petition, the estimated value of the estate property is $0. What is the purpose of opening this probate?
